description: Regulatory Success Matrix for Gateway 2 Submissions - Proprietary assessment framework synthesised from BSR corpus

matrix.matrix_title: Fire Strategy Report Present and Complete
matrix.matrix_description: A comprehensive fire strategy is fundamental to demonstrating compliance with Requirements B1-B5. BSR expects this as a core Gateway 2 document.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes a fire strategy report that addresses means of escape, compartmentation, external fire spread, structural fire resistance, and firefighter access. Document is clearly titled and authored by a competent fire engineer.
failure_modes: No fire strategy report present
Fire strategy exists but missing key sections (e.g., no compartmentation)
Fire strategy present but unsigned or no author credentials
Fire strategy references outdated standards

matrix.matrix_description: Approved Document B requires clear demonstration of escape routes, travel distances, and exit capacities. Unclear escape provisions are a common rejection reason.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy explicitly states evacuation strategy type, travel distances with measurements, exit widths, protected stairways, and compliance route (ADB or fire engineering).
failure_modes: No evacuation strategy type stated
Travel distances mentioned but not quantified
No reference to stair protection or capacity
Relies on fire engineering without stating approach

matrix.matrix_title: Compartmentation Strategy with Fire Resistance Periods
matrix.matrix_description: Buildings must be divided into compartments to inhibit fire spread. BSR expects explicit fire resistance periods aligned with building height and purpose group.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy defines compartment sizes, fire resistance periods (e.g., 60/90/120 minutes) for walls and floors, and fire stopping approach. References ADB Table A1/A2 or equivalent.
failure_modes: No compartmentation section
Compartments described but no fire resistance periods
Fire resistance stated but not justified against building height
No fire stopping strategy

matrix.matrix_title: External Wall System Fire Performance Specified
matrix.matrix_description: Post-Grenfell requirements mandate detailed specification of external wall materials. This is a Gateway 2 priority check area.
matrix.success_definition: External wall schedule or specification lists all materials, fire classifications (A1/A2/B etc), insulation types, cavity barriers, and compliance route. For 18m+ buildings, explicitly addresses the ban on combustible materials.
failure_modes: No external wall schedule
Materials listed but no fire classifications
No mention of cavity barriers
Claims compliance without specifying materials
Uses generic descriptions (e.g., 'fire-rated cladding') without specifics

matrix.matrix_description: For residential buildings over 11m, sprinklers are typically required. BSR expects clear statement of sprinkler strategy.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy confirms whether sprinklers are provided, states coverage (full/partial), references relevant standard (BS 9251 or BS EN 12845), and explains design intent.
failure_modes: No mention of sprinklers in fire strategy
States 'sprinklers to be provided' without specification
Height over 11m but no sprinkler justification
Sprinkler design not referenced against standards

matrix.matrix_description: Buildings over 18m require firefighting shafts and enhanced access. BSR scrutinises this for HRB applications.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy addresses firefighting shaft provision (if applicable), fire main locations, vehicle access, and coordination with local fire service. For 18m+ buildings, explicitly describes shaft design.
failure_modes: No firefighting access section
Over 18m but no firefighting shaft mentioned
Generic statement without specifics
No evidence of fire service consultation

matrix.matrix_title: Second Staircase Provision for Tall Residential
matrix.matrix_description: Recent guidance strengthens second staircase requirements for residential buildings over 18m. This is a key review focus.
matrix.success_definition: For residential HRBs, fire strategy explicitly addresses number of stairs, justification if single stair proposed, or confirmation of two-stair design with protected lobbies.
failure_modes: No mention of staircase strategy
Single stair proposed without fire engineering justification
Two stairs shown but no protected lobby approach
Height over 18m but stair count unclear

matrix.matrix_description: Gateway 2 requires demonstration that structural design meets Building Regulations. Absence of structural information is a completeness gap.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes structural report or statement from chartered structural engineer covering foundation approach, superstructure system, and key design standards (Eurocodes).
failure_modes: No structural documentation
Structural drawings only without report
No engineer credentials evident
References outdated codes

matrix.matrix_title: Structural Fire Resistance Aligned with Fire Strategy
matrix.matrix_description: Structural elements must achieve fire resistance periods consistent with the fire strategy. Misalignment is a common issue.
matrix.success_definition: Structural report references fire resistance requirements and confirms structural frame achieves required periods (e.g., 120 minutes for >30m buildings). Cross-references fire strategy.
failure_modes: No fire resistance mentioned in structural report
Fire resistance periods differ from fire strategy
No cross-reference between structural and fire documents
Fire resistance stated but not justified

matrix.matrix_title: Principal Designer Identified with Competence Evidence
matrix.matrix_description: Building Safety Act requires a competent Principal Designer for HRBs. Gateway 2 applications must demonstrate this appointment.
matrix.success_definition: Pack clearly names Principal Designer, states their organisation and qualifications, and evidences their competence for the building type (e.g., HRB experience, professional registration).
failure_modes: No Principal Designer named
Named but no credentials or qualifications
Role assigned but competence not evidenced
Unclear whether PD has HRB-specific competence

matrix.matrix_title: Principal Contractor Identified with Competence Evidence
matrix.matrix_description: Building Safety Act requires a competent Principal Contractor for HRBs. Appointment must be demonstrated at Gateway 2.
matrix.success_definition: Pack clearly names Principal Contractor (or confirms appointment pending), states their organisation, and evidences competence for HRB construction.
failure_modes: No Principal Contractor named or referenced
Named but no competence evidence
States 'TBC' without explaining appointment process
Competence claimed but not substantiated

matrix.matrix_description: HRBs must maintain a golden thread of building information. Gateway 2 should demonstrate awareness and approach.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes statement or section on information management approach, acknowledges golden thread requirements, and describes how information will be structured, stored, and handed over.
failure_modes: No mention of golden thread or information management
Acknowledges requirement but no approach described
Vague statements without practical detail
No mention of digital format or accessibility

matrix.matrix_description: HRB construction requires formal change control with BSR notification for major changes. Gateway 2 should show this is planned.
matrix.success_definition: Pack describes approach to managing design changes during construction, references need to notify BSR of major changes, and identifies responsibility for change assessment.
failure_modes: No mention of change control
Generic quality management without BSR-specific process
No acknowledgment of major change notification requirement
Unclear who assesses change significance

matrix.matrix_description: DAS provides essential context about design intent and decisions. Its absence makes it harder for BSR to understand the proposal.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes Design and Access Statement that describes the building, its context, design rationale, and access provisions for occupants and emergency services.
failure_modes: No DAS present
DAS present but very brief or lacking substance
Design described but no access considerations
No explanation of key design decisions

matrix.matrix_description: Mechanical, electrical and plumbing systems affect building safety. Gateway 2 benefits from clear MEP information.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes MEP specification or report covering heating, ventilation, electrical systems, and plumbing. Shows coordination with fire and structural strategies.
failure_modes: No MEP documentation
MEP mentioned in other docs but no dedicated specification
Specification exists but lacks system details
No evidence of coordination with fire strategy

matrix.matrix_description: Approved Document F sets ventilation requirements. Submissions should demonstrate compliant ventilation approach.
matrix.success_definition: Pack specifies ventilation strategy (natural, mechanical, or mixed-mode), ventilation rates for habitable rooms, and compliance route with ADF.
failure_modes: No ventilation information
Mentions 'mechanical ventilation' without rates
No reference to ADF or equivalent standards
Ventilation appears in passing but not substantively addressed

matrix.matrix_title: Fire Detection and Alarm System Specified
matrix.matrix_description: Fire detection is a core life safety system. BSR expects clear specification of detection approach.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy or MEP specification states fire alarm category (L1/L2/L3 per BS 5839), detector types, coverage, and integration with evacuation strategy.
failure_modes: No fire alarm information
Generic 'fire alarm system' without category
Category stated but coverage unclear
No link to evacuation strategy

matrix.matrix_description: Common areas and escape routes typically need smoke control. This should be addressed in fire strategy.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy describes smoke control approach for common corridors and stairs (natural, mechanical, or pressure differential), references relevant standards, and coordinates with compartmentation.
failure_modes: No smoke control mentioned
Smoke control referenced but not specified
Strategy unclear (e.g., 'smoke vents provided' without detail)
No coordination with stair pressurisation if claimed

matrix.matrix_description: Residential buildings require clear evacuation strategy (simultaneous, phased, or stay-put). Ambiguity causes review delays.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy explicitly states evacuation strategy type, justifies choice based on building design, and explains how it will be communicated to residents.
failure_modes: No evacuation strategy stated
Assumes stay-put without stating it
Strategy stated but not justified
Mixed messages between documents

matrix.matrix_description: Inconsistent building heights across documents suggest coordination failures and undermine confidence in the submission.
matrix.success_definition: Building height is stated consistently (within reasonable tolerance) across fire strategy, structural report, DAS, and other documents.
failure_modes: Different heights in different documents
Height stated in some documents but not others
Measurement basis unclear (to roof, to parapet, etc.)
Significant discrepancies (>1m)

matrix.matrix_description: Inconsistent storey counts suggest coordination failures. This affects HRB classification.
matrix.success_definition: Number of storeys is stated consistently across all documents, with clear basis (above ground, including basement, etc.).
failure_modes: Different storey counts in different documents
Basis of count unclear (some include basement, some don't)
Storey count affects HRB threshold but inconsistent

matrix.matrix_description: FRA provides risk-based foundation for fire strategy. Its presence strengthens submissions.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes Fire Risk Assessment that identifies building-specific fire risks, assesses likelihood and consequence, and links to mitigation measures in fire strategy.
failure_modes: No FRA present
FRA present but generic/template-based
FRA doesn't reference the specific building
FRA not linked to fire strategy measures

matrix.matrix_description: London Plan Policy D12(B) requires major developments to submit a Fire Statement prepared by a qualified fire engineer.
matrix.success_definition: For London projects, pack includes a dedicated Fire Statement (distinct from fire strategy) addressing Policy D12 requirements, prepared by a suitably qualified professional.
failure_modes: No Fire Statement (London project)
Fire strategy exists but not in Fire Statement format
Fire Statement present but author qualifications unclear
Doesn't address D12-specific requirements

matrix.matrix_description: London Fire Safety LPG emphasises Personal Emergency Evacuation Plans for disabled residents. This should be addressed for residential HRBs.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy or Fire Statement acknowledges PEEP requirements, describes how evacuation of mobility-impaired residents will be managed, and identifies refuge areas if applicable.
failure_modes: No mention of PEEPs or disabled evacuation
Generic accessibility statement without evacuation specifics
Refuges shown but not explained
PEEP mentioned but no management approach

matrix.matrix_description: Well-coordinated submissions show documents referencing each other. This indicates design team coordination.
matrix.success_definition: Fire strategy references structural report, MEP references fire strategy, DAS references key technical documents. Drawing numbers are cited where relevant.
failure_modes: Documents appear isolated/standalone
No cross-references between technical documents
References are generic ('as per architect drawings') not specific
Referenced documents not included in pack

matrix.matrix_description: Explicit mapping of design to regulations helps BSR understand compliance approach. Its absence requires more BSR analysis.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes compliance statement or matrix showing how Building Regulations (Schedule 1) requirements are addressed, with document references.
failure_modes: No compliance mapping
Compliance claimed but not demonstrated
Only fire regulations addressed, others ignored
Generic compliance statement without specifics

matrix.matrix_description: Version control supports golden thread requirements and shows document maturity.
matrix.success_definition: Documents include revision numbers, dates, and where applicable, revision history. Superseded versions are clearly marked.
failure_modes: No version numbers on documents
Dates present but no revision tracking
Inconsistent versioning approach across documents
Draft watermarks on submitted documents

matrix.matrix_description: Gateway 2 should demonstrate how construction will be controlled. This supports confidence in delivery.
matrix.success_definition: Pack indicates approach to construction phase management, site supervision, and quality assurance. May reference future Construction Phase Plan.
failure_modes: No construction management information
Design documents only with no delivery context
Vague statements about 'quality construction'
No indication of how design intent will be maintained

matrix.matrix_description: A clear building description helps BSR understand the proposal quickly. Ambiguity slows review.
matrix.success_definition: Pack provides clear description of building including: use, height, storeys, units/area, construction type, key features. Information is consistent and easy to find.
failure_modes: Building description scattered across documents
Key parameters missing (units, area)
Description inconsistent between documents
Technical jargon without plain language summary

matrix.matrix_description: Understanding site context helps BSR assess external fire spread, access, and environmental factors.
matrix.success_definition: Pack includes site plan, describes surrounding context, identifies access routes, and notes any site-specific constraints affecting fire safety or construction.
failure_modes: No site plan
Site plan without context description
Access routes not shown
Adjacent buildings not considered for fire spread
